pain_point_label: Low Parental Awareness of School Supports, Programs, & College Pathways

matches.match_rationale: The pain point explicitly identifies top-down, one-size-fits-all school marketing as the failure mode. AI text generation can produce targeted, segmented parent communications — differentiated by scholarship status, rural vs. urban household, grade level, or language — that explain tutoring, co-curricular programs, financial aid, and college prep pathways in accessible, culturally resonant language. This directly replaces the poster-and-presentation broadcast model with relevant, personalized messaging.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An AI-driven parent communication engine that auto-generates weekly or event-triggered messages (email, SMS, WhatsApp) tailored to each family's profile — scholarship eligibility, student grade, expressed interests — covering program spotlights, deadlines, and success stories from similar families.

matches.match_rationale: Rural parents with misconceptions about tuition, curriculum, or graduation requirements need accurate, on-demand answers grounded in the school's actual policies and program catalog — not generic web results. RAG over the school's official documents (program guides, scholarship handbooks, college prep resources) lets parents ask plain-language questions and receive precise, cited answers any time of day, bridging the information gap without requiring staff availability.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A parent-facing 'school information assistant' (web or WhatsApp bot) that answers questions like 'What scholarships does my child qualify for?' or 'Is college prep tutoring free?' by retrieving answers directly from the school's own program and policy documents, with citations parents can verify.

matches.match_rationale: Word-of-mouth is the only effective channel precisely because parents need a two-way dialogue — they have misconceptions they need corrected and specific questions about their child's situation. A conversational agent can replicate the trusted peer-referral dynamic at scale, engaging parents in multi-turn conversations to surface relevant programs, clarify financial aid myths, and guide them through college prep steps based on their child's grade and goals.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A multilingual parent engagement chatbot (available via school app or WhatsApp) that proactively messages families with personalised program recommendations and responds to follow-up questions across multiple turns — effectively replicating the trusted peer referral channel for families who lack that network.

matches.match_rationale: The core failure is that marginalized families are not reached by word-of-mouth networks that organically surface relevant programs to connected families. A recommendation system can proactively surface the right programs, resources, and college prep steps to each family based on their student's profile (grade, academic standing, scholarship status, interests) — without requiring parents to know what to search for or who to ask.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A recommendation layer within the parent portal that surfaces 'Programs your child may benefit from' — e.g., pushing tutoring options to students with declining grades, financial aid info to scholarship families, or college prep workshops to 10th-grade parents — based on student profile signals rather than passive parent browsing.
